_readme: Persistent action-items tracker for Clio. One row per tracked issue/decision. 'status' is the current state; 'comment' holds ONLY the latest status/action note (no history needed — full history lives in the conversation transcript). Update this file the instant any item's status changes — do not batch. Add new items the moment they're raised, even if not yet investigated.

items.item: Double-charge risk
items.description: A call ending manually and automatically at the same time could deduct minutes twice.
items.status: fixed, deployed
items.comment: Closed via cycle-scoped billing math plus billing off Hume's own call duration as source of truth. Live in production.

items.item: Session length field is overloaded
items.description: One field stored both the plan and the actual usage, overwriting the plan every time a call ends.
items.status: fixed
items.comment: Split into a separate planned-length field and actual-usage field. Migration applied. Historical sessions left as unknown, only new sessions affected.

items.item: A finished session can be rejoined
items.description: Nothing stopped re-entering a call that already ended — the root enabler of the field-overload issue above.
items.status: fixed
items.comment: Added a check that blocks rejoining a session once it's marked completed.

items.item: Test call ended after only ~2:41
items.description: A call ended from the user side, not by Clio deciding the session was done.
items.status: backlog — discuss later
items.comment: Moved to backlog per Arun's direction 2026-07-06 morning. Not actively worked until revisited.

items.item: Missing background material
items.description: Every session showed a placeholder instead of real subject background.
items.status: fixed
items.comment: Real background content now generated and wired in before every session.

items.item: Character-limit warning in Hume's UI
items.description: A character-limit warning appeared in Hume's dashboard.
items.status: resolved, safeguard added
items.comment: Confirmed real limit, but only affects a voice-tone layer we don't rely on. Added a guardrail to stay under it regardless.

items.item: No proper greeting at session open
items.description: Clio doesn't open the session herself — she reacts to the participant speaking first instead of greeting them by name.
items.status: fixed, deployed, confirmed working
items.comment: Confirmed by Arun on a real live call 2026-07-07: Clio now opens the conversation herself and greets him by name. Root cause was a leftover technical message from the old voice system blocking her own greeting; fixed by skipping it specifically for the new system, with the signed-up user's first name threaded in. Closed.

items.item: Abrupt call ending, no graceful wrap-up
items.description: Calls ended abruptly with no summary or goodbye.
items.status: fixed
items.comment: Added a live wrap-up nudge near the end plus a permanent closing-behavior rule (summary then goodbye). Deployed.

items.item: Web search tool sometimes missing
items.description: The web search capability worked sometimes and silently disappeared other times.
items.status: fixed
items.comment: Root cause was a code bug reading tools dynamically from the wrong shape; fixed and confirmed present on a real live call.

items.item: A third capability is silently missing
items.description: A third custom tool (likely a way to gracefully defer a question) exists on the account but never gets carried into new sessions.
items.status: fixed, deployed — needs a real call to fully confirm
items.comment: Shipped 2026-07-06 per DEFER-QUESTION-01. The Hume-native prompt now tells Clio to verbally acknowledge deferring a question with no tool call, using natural phrasing anchored on 'next time'/'next session'/'cover that properly'. The old ElevenLabs-path prompt was also updated similarly but is a separate, untouched path. inngest/session-quality-evaluator.ts has two detectors, gated by session.hume_native_enabled. Both write to the same existing sessions.deferred_questions column, same existing UI. 7 new unit tests passing, full suite 420/422 (2 pre-existing unrelated failures untouched). Live in production — needs one real live Hume-native session where Clio actually defers something to fully confirm end-to-end.

items.item: Some settings reported back oddly
items.description: One data point suggested a setting mismatch, but it came from an error response, not a real call.
items.status: P1 — backlog, after Arun finishes testing
items.comment: Not yet logged/investigated with a clear record. Needs a fresh check against a real call before trusting it. Marked P1 by Arun 2026-07-06 — investigate/build once he's done with his current testing pass.

items.item: Overnight backup / config-checking tool
items.description: The nightly job that archives and cleans up session configs was broken because a database table it needed was never created.
items.status: deployed to production, awaiting first real overnight run
items.comment: Confirmed live in production (checked 2026-07-06 morning — current deployed build includes this fix and is several commits further ahead). Just hasn't had its first real overnight run since going live to fully confirm end to end.

items.item: Automated quality check flagged a call
items.description: Our own quality system flagged that no proper coaching speech was detected on one test call.
items.status: P1 — backlog, after Arun finishes testing
items.comment: Still open. Needs a deeper look on its own. Marked P1 by Arun 2026-07-06 — investigate/build once he's done with his current testing pass.

items.item: "Unknown session" names in history
items.description: A separate, pre-existing bug quietly stripping session names out of the learner's history.
items.status: P1 — backlog, after Arun finishes testing
items.comment: Confirmed unrelated to recent changes. Also confirmed it leaks into what Clio reads before a real call, not just a display issue. Marked P1 by Arun 2026-07-06 — investigate/build once he's done with his current testing pass.

items.item: Verified-minute billing silently falling back
items.description: Billing was asking Hume for the real call length too soon, before Hume finished recording it, and silently falling back to a less accurate method.
items.status: fixed, deployed
items.comment: Now waits, and tries once more, before falling back. Covered by a new automated test (6 scenarios, all passing). Live in production. Superseded/complemented by hume-ground-truth-webhook below, which removes the wait entirely when it fires.

items.item: Screen stuck / missing Overview and Summary screens
items.description: The shared screen got stuck on the opening title card for an entire call. Root cause: the Overview screen never existed in the live call's screen list at all, so every topic displayed one slot ahead of what was being said.
items.status: fixed, deployed
items.comment: Added real Overview and Summary screens into every place that builds a session's screen list (3 places found and aligned). Skipped topics correctly excluded from the summary. Type-checks clean, no regressions found. Live in production — however, a separate live-conductor shadowing bug meant this screen wasn't actually reaching users until that bug was found and fixed on 2026-07-06; it was not previously tested/confirmed working as this comment had claimed.

items.item: Contradictory closing instructions
items.description: One instruction told Clio to ask "any more questions?" and wait; another said to skip that and call a tool that no longer exists.
items.status: fixed
items.comment: Removed the stale instruction so only the correct, newer closing behavior remains.

items.item: Script sounded written, not spoken
items.description: The material Clio was told to say read like a dense written article rather than natural spoken coaching.
items.status: fixed (scoped)
items.comment: Added explicit "sound spoken, not written" guidance to the content generator. Scoped instruction addition, not a full pipeline rewrite.

items.item: Same check-in phrase repeated every section
items.description: The fallback verification question repeated verbatim every section.
items.status: fixed
items.comment: Now rotates through 4 natural variants instead of repeating the same line.

items.item: Retake a completed session
items.description: A completed session can't be rejoined (by design, to prevent double-charging). Arun wants a way for the user to take that session's material again as a brand-new attempt — new session, new call, new billing, same topic.
items.status: removed — Arun decided this was unnecessary complexity, superseded by simply removing the restart block instead
items.comment: 2026-07-06: Arun reviewed this and the underlying restart block together and decided both should go. The 'Retake this session' button/modal/API route (app/api/sessions/[id]/retake/route.ts) were deleted; the retaken_from_session_id column is left in place, unused, no down-migration. See REVERT-01 below and docs/specs/REVERT-01-remove-restart-block-and-retake.md. Historical spec/brief kept at .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/RETAKE-01-*.md and retake-completed-session.md for record.

items.item: Removed the hard block on restarting a completed session
items.description: SESSION-DURATION-01 added a guard that flatly blocked ever restarting a session once status === 'completed', on top of the actual root-cause fix (a calculation-scoping correction in computeBilledMinutes()). Arun decided the guard was an unnecessary safety net, not a requirement — entering a meeting URL and clicking the normal launch flow on a completed session should just work again.
items.status: done, deployed
items.comment: 2026-07-06: removed the 409 SESSION_ALREADY_COMPLETED guard from app/api/sessions/[id]/start/route.ts. A completed session now goes through the identical start path as a scheduled one (status -> active, audit token, bot_joined event); plan-approval and minutes-balance checks unchanged. The real root-cause fix in lib/session-billing.ts (computeBilledMinutes()) was left completely untouched — still load-bearing for the Hume-native fallback path. This also let RETAKE-01 be removed entirely (see above). Live in production — ready to test.

items.item: Instant, exact billing via Hume's own end-of-call webhook
items.description: Today, billing waits ~3-7 seconds after a call ends and asks Hume for the call length (with one retry if Hume hasn't finalized it yet), only falling back to our own estimate if that fails. This adds a faster, more direct path: Hume can proactively tell us the second a call ends, so we don't have to wait and ask.
items.status: deployed, migration applied, secret set — needs 1 live test to confirm the webhook actually registers
items.comment: 2026-07-06: CEO-agent code review completed. Confirmed correct: signature verified before trusting any data, stale-webhook-row protection reuses the proven cycle-scoping logic, and the existing wait-and-ask billing path is completely unchanged if this fast path doesn't fire — cannot make billing worse. Migration 062 applied, HUME_WEBHOOK_SECRET set in Vercel Production, code deployed. ONE OPEN ITEM: the exact field/shape for registering the webhook with Hume is a well-reasoned best guess, not yet round-trip confirmed against Hume's real API. Confirm on the next real test call by checking whether a 'hume_webhook_chat_ended' audit row appears for that session — if not, billing still works exactly as before, just without the speed-up yet.

items.item: A call ending via the meeting-recording tool's own detection skips billing entirely
items.description: When the meeting-recording tool notices everyone left the call and marks the session done on its own, it never goes through the billing process at all — no minutes counted, no deduction, planned length left stale on screen. Found while investigating why a real ~2.4 minute test call showed 15 minutes and deducted nothing.
items.status: backlog — deferred, discuss later
items.comment: Pre-existing bug, not caused by anything built recently (confirmed via git history — predates the current billing system entirely, just never got connected to it). Confirmed Arun was undercharged on a real test call (2.4 real minutes, 0 deducted). Fix identified: route this path through the same 'properly close out a call' function everything else already uses (already safe to call twice). Deferred by Arun 2026-07-07 — 'we will come to this billing question later.' Also logged in BACKLOG.md (RECALL-WEBHOOK-BILLING-GAP). Not built. Whether to correct Arun's balance for the undercharged call is also still open.

items.item: Session length: fix fallback logic going forward (no historical repair)
items.description: Some places in the code checked a session's length without preferring the user's originally chosen plan length, and content generation silently assumed 30 minutes when that value was missing instead of using the user's real preference.
items.status: fixed, deployed — ready to test on any new session
items.comment: Went through full CEO -> BA -> approval -> build. Fixed 4 spots: the single-session lookup, the pre-call balance check, the scheduling lookup, and content generation's silent 30-min fallback (now uses the user's real chosen preference first, 30 only as an absolute last resort). Confirmed the core logic that decides how many sessions a topic needs was untouched and was already correct. No historical session data touched or repaired, by explicit design. Live in production.

items.item: Overview screen skipped instantly + content silently marked ready when it wasn't
items.description: The Overview screen had no real prepared content and no instruction telling Clio to slow down there, so she moved on almost instantly. Separately, one specific new topic's content was never actually generated, but the system still reported it as ready, so Clio taught from her own general knowledge instead of the real prepared material.
items.status: fixed, deployed — ready to test
items.comment: Went through full CEO -> BA -> approval -> build. Root cause for the false-ready bug: there were two separate places that could mark a session's content ready, and only one of them actually checked its work — the other could save 'ready' before its own double-check ran, and never corrected it if that check later failed. Fixed by making one single shared checkpoint that all of them must pass before ever saying ready. Also gave the Overview and Summary screens real content (built from the session's own real title/agenda/topics, not another AI pass) and told Clio to treat the Overview with the same care as any other topic. No historical/already-broken session repaired, by design — going forward only. Live in production — not yet observed on a real live call.

items.item: Test toggle: skip pre-built content entirely, generate each screen live one at a time
items.description: A new toggle so a session can start straight into the Overview with no pre-built content check at all, then generate each next topic's content live, only once the current one is done — instead of using anything pre-built.
items.status: deployed, but confirmed still incomplete — re-broke as a full call termination on 2026-07-08, see session-terminates-after-overview above
items.comment: CORRECTION 2026-07-08: this entry previously said 'uncommitted locally, awaiting deploy' -- that was stale. Verified via git log: the fix is commit 38ba404, already an ancestor of the exact commit (bb797c2) running in the production deployment (dpl_3nqoBvxjPmbASUavK5qUoi2qSyfU) Arun tested against on 2026-07-08. Original fix: (1) wired the 'generate next topic live' advance_tab handler into the real voice system's tool-call handling (POST /api/live-conductor/advance-tab), (2) corrected contradictory prompt wording. CONFIRMED BY TODAY'S LIVE TEST that this did not fully close the gap: advance_tab was never called once during the whole session (zero log hits), and show_visual's own clamping logic was never updated for on-demand mode, so Clio still ran out of real content right after the Overview -- now escalated from 'screen looks stuck' to 'the whole call ends', because of the separately-shipped graceful-auto-hangup behavior. Full root cause + fix direction in session-terminates-after-overview above and .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/ONDEMAND-02-session-ends-after-overview.md.

items.item: Screen falls behind voice / stuck 'unable to connect' error
items.description: When Clio moves through screens quickly, an earlier one could get silently skipped and everything after ends up one step behind what she's saying. Separately, the 'unable to connect' error stayed on screen forever, even after the call actually recovered.
items.status: fixed, deployed — needs a real live call to confirm
items.comment: Went through full CEO -> BA -> approval -> build, with a same-day self-caught correction before ship (see below). Fix 1: every screen still gets guaranteed a moment on screen before the next one can replace it (rapid-fire transitions still spaced out), so nothing gets silently skipped. Fix 2: the stuck error still clears itself automatically once the connection has been genuinely stable again. CORRECTION (caught in CEO review before ship): the first-pass implementation of Fix 1 had `await screenQueueRef.current` on the LLM-facing return path at both call sites, which meant the 1500ms minimum-display floor blocked Clio's response to the LLM on EVERY screen change, not just rapid-fire ones. Corrected: the queue assignment still happens synchronously so writes still serialize and rapid-fire calls still get spaced out, but the function no longer awaits the queue before returning instruction text to the LLM — the DB write now happens in the background. Deliberate tradeoff, judged acceptable: the screen update can now land a few hundred ms after Clio starts speaking about it, instead of being guaranteed to land first. npx tsc --noEmit clean. Deployed 2026-07-07, commit 924b5b0, Vercel Production READY, homepage 200, zero new runtime errors in the 15 min after deploy. Needs one real live call from Arun to fully confirm both fixes work end-to-end.

items.item: Clio (Hume) restarted mid-call
items.description: During Arun's 2026-07-07 live test (session 343baf89-a1a9-4a72-bdfb-6373a08ae043), the voice assistant restarted partway through the call. A fresh /api/hume-native/session-chat-id call fired at 21:06:57 UTC (a new Hume chat being created — consistent with a restart), and /api/webhooks/hume returned repeated 400 responses both right after session start (20:57:52-20:58:08) and right around the restart (21:06:53-21:07:13). Not yet root-caused.
items.status: root-caused — not a crash, existing reconnect logic worked as designed; underlying disconnect cause still unknown
items.comment: Re-verified 2026-07-07 with fresh log pulls + source reads (this session): pulled /api/webhooks/hume runtime logs for both the 20:57:45-20:58:15 session-start window (3 hits, all 400) and the 21:06:40-21:07:20 restart window (6 hits, all 400) — identical cadence and status in both, no burst or spike at the restart moment, confirming this endpoint is constant background noise unrelated to the restart. Cross-checked against get_runtime_errors for the same deployment/window: zero thrown exceptions. Searched runtime logs for any [hume-webhook] console.error/warn output (the only log lines route.ts emits) in the full 20:57:40-21:07:20 range: none found, meaning the 400s are the SILENT early-return branches in app/api/webhooks/hume/route.ts ("Missing signature headers" line 66, or "Invalid signature" line 82) — not a parse failure, not a DB failure, not a thrown error. route.ts's own header comment (lines 20-27) already flags the HMAC signed-string format as an unverified guess pending a real Hume test delivery, which fits: the signature check appears to never have passed, at any point in this call, not just during the restart. Root cause of the 400s = pre-existing, unrelated to the restart. For the restart itself: confirmed the surrounding request timeline (21:06:53-21:07:15) shows this session running on the ElevenLabs path (repeated /api/attendee/webhook calls with live transcript text, e.g. "Yeah, I think it should start with the first one."), while /api/hume-native/session-chat-id returned 200 at 21:06:57. That route (read in full) is called from exactly one place — the Hume onConnect callback in WalkthroughClient.tsx line 638-644, gated by HUME_NATIVE_ENABLED — so a 200 there at 21:06:57 does mean some Hume connection object completed (re)connection at that moment. Read the onDisconnect/onConnect reconnect logic in WalkthroughClient.tsx (Hume block ~line 631-696, ElevenLabs block ~line 1053-1107): both implement identical onDisconnect -> backoff retry -> connect() -> fresh onConnect pattern, consistent with the 21:06:57 call being that recovery path completing, not a fresh unexplained crash. STILL UNRESOLVED, honestly: (1) I cannot confirm from Vercel data alone whether a Hume connection was genuinely live in parallel with the ElevenLabs-dominant traffic in this session, since I found no code path in the file segments I read that runs both providers concurrently — this needs a direct check of how HUME_NATIVE_ENABLED / provider selection is decided for this specific session, which I have not done. (2) The actual WebSocket close code/reason that triggered onDisconnect is logged only to the browser/Attendee-bot console via console.log/console.error in WalkthroughClient.tsx — Vercel runtime logs never capture this, by design (they only capture server-side execution: API routes and middleware). There is no Vercel query that can recover it. Closing this fully requires either a repro with browser DevTools open, or forwarding that client-side onError/onDisconnect payload to a server endpoint so it lands in logs we can query.

items.item: Call ends right after the Overview instead of continuing
items.description: Arun tested a live call 2026-07-08: as soon as the Overview finished, he saw a 'session complete' visual and the bot disconnected from the call — the session terminated entirely instead of moving into the rest of the agenda.
items.status: root-caused — Feature Brief written, awaiting BA spec
items.comment: CONFIRMED with logs + DB, not a guess: the specific test session (2f04bdb6-1bfd-459c-b6bd-e5b2755a2db5) had the LIVE_CONDUCTOR_ONDEMAND_TEST toggle active. Vercel logs for that exact call (POST /api/recall/bot, 01:16:24 UTC) show the on-demand code path deliberately discarding all 4 already-generated real subtopics (confirmed present in sessions.sub_sessions and live_conductor_content.tabs) and writing an Overview-ONLY 1-element sections array (app/api/recall/bot/route.ts lines 369-410, ONDEMAND-01 block). Clio's own Hume-native prompt therefore had zero real subtopic content to teach. The mechanism meant to fetch the next topic live mid-call (advance_tab -> POST /api/live-conductor/advance-tab) was never called once in the entire session (full log search of the call window, zero hits) -- only one scroll_to 0 (the Overview) ever fired, then end-call ~104s later. With nothing left in her context, Clio followed the already-shipped graceful-closing rule (prompt-template.ts rule 8, literal example 'Take care, talk soon.') which contains 'take care' -- one of WalkthroughClient.tsx's own FAREWELL_PHRASES -- and/or triggered Hume's own native hang_up builtin tool; either ends the call. Cross-checked against the automated post-call quality evaluator: this exact session was independently flagged quality_error='no_clio_speech_detected', consistent with no real coaching content ever being delivered. This is the SAME defect family as ondemand-live-screen-generation-toggle below (commit 38ba404, confirmed live in the exact production deployment Arun tested against) -- that fix wired advance_tab's server round-trip but never gave Clio's prompt an unambiguous on-demand-mode instruction to call it (vs show_visual, which silently clamps back to the Overview when the sections array is 1-element), and never made show_visual itself trigger on-demand generation the way the separate REALTIME_VISUAL_TEST_MODE branch does. Scope: LIVE_CONDUCTOR_ONDEMAND_TEST / NEXT_PUBLIC_LIVE_CONDUCTOR_ENABLED are test-only toggles, off by default -- zero impact to real user sessions today, only to Arun's own on-demand-mode testing. Independent finding flagged alongside this: FAREWELL_PHRASES also contains generic phrases ('we're done', 'all done', 'great work today', 'well done today') that could false-positive on ANY natural section-transition speech, not just this bug -- worth a separate hardening look regardless. Feature Brief written: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/ONDEMAND-02-session-ends-after-overview.md. Awaiting BA spec before any code changes (no code changed in this investigation).

items.item: End-of-session detection: replace phrase-matching with an explicit tool-call signal (SESSION-END-01)
items.description: Arun chose Option B for the FAREWELL_PHRASES fragility flagged alongside session-terminates-after-overview: after a wrap-up recap and confirming nothing further, Clio must trigger an explicit end_session tool call as the authoritative end-of-session signal, not transcript phrase-matching.
items.status: fixed, deployed 2026-07-09 — commit 1f56d36
items.comment: Feature Brief: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/SESSION-END-01-explicit-end-session-signal.md. Requirement Document (all 12 sections, Section 11 empty): .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/SESSION-END-01-explicit-end-session-signal.md. Key finding: end_session is NOT a new mechanism — it already exists and already works as the authoritative signal for the standard pipeline (ElevenLabs + Hume-Custom-LLM, via lib/clio-context-builder.ts / lib/voice/live-conductor-bridge.ts / lib/voice/relay-handler.ts), and WalkthroughClient.tsx already has working client handlers for it (line 826 Hume, line 902 ElevenLabs). The only gap is the Hume-native path (NEXT_PUBLIC_HUME_NATIVE_ENABLED): its prompt never instructs calling end_session, and the tool is not yet registered on Hume-native's base config. Resolved decisively for a NEW custom end_session tool over reusing Hume's native hang_up builtin: hang_up has no client-side handler anywhere in this codebase and fires through HumeAdapter's generic ws.onclose path — identical to an ordinary drop/reconnect, with no event.code/event.reason branching to tell them apart (independently re-verified by the CEO agent against the actual source, not taken on the BA's word). end_session by contrast fires through a named, structured tool_call message, fully decoupled from connection close. Also closes a hole the original brief hadn't anticipated: hang_up must be explicitly filtered out of Hume-native's builtin_tools (config-provisioner.ts) or it remains a live, invisible alternate way to end the call even after end_session is wired in. FAREWELL_PHRASES is kept but demoted to a gated, final-section-only, 8-second-grace-window fallback (never armed during ordinary mid-session wrap-up chatter — closes the original false-positive risk). Scope: Hume-native prompt/tool changes only; ElevenLabs/Custom-LLM untouched (already correct). Built, independently verified (tsc clean, diff scope matched exactly), committed and deployed to production 2026-07-09 (commit 1f56d36). One dependency remains for full activation: the one-time Hume Tools API registration (2 API calls) using the real (non-placeholder) HUME_API_KEY — until Arun runs this, Hume-native sessions fall back to the demoted FAREWELL_PHRASES timer, which is already a strict improvement over the pre-existing behavior. Process note: a message relayed via the orchestrator mid-investigation claimed Arun already preferred hang_up; per standing practice this was not treated as authoritative, the BA resolved on evidence regardless, and the discrepancy was reported to Arun directly — he confirmed end_session was correct once shown the evidence.

items.item: Replace Hume-native's full pre-written script with a compact live-teaching summary
items.description: Arun's decision 2026-07-08: for Hume-native voice sessions only, replace buildSessionScript()'s full pre-written TEACH text + 7 hardcoded checkpoint response variants with a compact per-topic summary, so Clio teaches live from the unchanged Topic Knowledge Base rather than reading a script. Gated behind a new env var, default OFF (today's full-script behavior unchanged), toggleable in production with no code rollback. Topic Context, pacing/time-budgets, and checkpoint-variant redesign are explicitly out of scope for this pass. Scope is Hume-native only (same precedent as SESSION-END-01) — ElevenLabs/Hume-Custom-LLM paths untouched.
items.status: fixed, deployed 2026-07-08 — commit 9aec10c, toggle flipped ON in production
items.comment: Feature Brief: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/SESSCTX-01-summary-driven-session-context.md. Requirement Document (all 12 sections, Section 11 empty): .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/SESSCTX-01-summary-driven-session-context.md. Design: new env var HUME_NATIVE_SUMMARY_MODE (server-only, default OFF, strict 'true' match). New sibling function buildSessionSummary() in lib/clio-context-builder.ts, alongside (not replacing) buildSessionScript() — CEO independently verified via grep that buildSessionScript() has exactly 3 call sites, all inside app/api/hume-native/provision-config/route.ts, and buildAllClioDocs() (the ElevenLabs/Hume-Custom-LLM/admin-QA path) is called only from 3 unrelated files that never touch provision-config/route.ts — confirming zero behavior change for those other callers. Only the real sessionContent assembly call site (~line 376-380) branches on the flag; the pre-check (~148-152) and recheck (~311-315) completeness-gate call sites deliberately stay unconditional on the existing buildSessionScript(), left as the more conservative (harder to false-positive) detector regardless of which mode ships to Clio. Summary text is extracted from the already-loaded trainingScripts[i] TEACH segment (sentence-boundary cap, ~40 words) — no new DB read, no new LLM call. Bookend sections (SessionOverview/SessionSummary) keep full script content unchanged in both modes, per existing prompt rules 1/8. Section-index header line for show_visual/advance_tab preserved byte-for-byte. Built, independently verified (tsc clean, 7/7 new unit tests passing, diff scope matched exactly), committed and deployed 2026-07-08 (commit 9aec10c). Arun then explicitly flipped HUME_NATIVE_SUMMARY_MODE=true in production the same day, per his standing preference to default new toggles ON so behavior is actually observable rather than left inert — this directly informed the RTV series brainstorm (docs/brainstorm-realtime-transcript-driven-visualization.md) that followed.

items.category: Template library review workflow (follow-on to RTV-04)
items.item: force_retrigger discarded the reviewer's feedback instead of forwarding it — every retry was guaranteed to fail
items.description: Second bug found on the same incident: after fixing the Inngest sync gap, Arun clicked Force retrigger on Overlay and the job ran (fix_attempt_count went 0→1) but immediately failed with 'No reviewer feedback was provided.' The nudge route's force_retrigger branch hardcoded notes: '' in the Inngest event instead of reading the row's own review_notes column, which still held Arun's full original feedback text.
items.status: fixed, deployed 2026-07-12 — commit 7c19152
items.comment: Feature Brief + Requirement Document: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/TMPL-04-force-retrigger-notes-fix.md, .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/TMPL-04-force-retrigger-notes-fix.md. TMPL-01's own spec never specified what notes value force_retrigger's event payload should carry, leaving this as an implicit assumption the build filled in with an empty string — reasonable-looking but wrong, since it defeats the entire purpose of a retry (nothing to act on). Fix: app/api/templates/library/[templateName]/route.ts's nudge handler extends its existing select to include review_notes and forwards it as-is in the inngest.send() call — no fallback substitution if it's genuinely null (a case that shouldn't be reachable via the UI today, since force_retrigger only appears once a fix cycle already exists, meaning a prior request_changes call must have already written review_notes). Caught the existing test (tests/integration/template-fix-nudge-api.test.ts) had encoded the OLD buggy behavior (notes: '') as the expected/correct assertion — updated it to assert the fixed behavior and added a new test for the null-passthrough edge case. Independently verified: tsc clean, all 10 tests in the nudge test file passing (8 original + 2 new), full suite passing (only the same pre-existing unrelated billing-test failure seen throughout this session), diff scope confirmed to touch only the nudge route and its own test file. Arun's original Overlay feedback ('increase canvas size, fix container overlap/margins, increase font size') is still intact on the row and will be forwarded correctly on his next force-retrigger click.

items.category: Template library review workflow (follow-on to RTV-04)
items.item: Overlay's fix cycle silently never ran — Inngest's function registry hadn't synced the new deployment
items.description: Arun submitted feedback on Overlay ('increase canvas size, fix container overlap/margins, increase font size') at 2026-07-11 21:46:58 UTC. The row correctly moved to changes_requested/fix_state=generating and the feedback_received log row was written, but no attempt_started event ever followed — fix_attempt_count stayed at 0 for over an hour. Arun reported no progress and asked for it to be checked.
items.status: root-caused and fixed 2026-07-11 — forced an Inngest re-sync against production, confirmed modified:true (registry really was stale). Awaiting Arun to click Force retrigger on Overlay's Fix Progress page to actually start the fix now that Inngest is listening.
items.comment: Root cause: templateFixGenerator was correctly imported and registered in app/api/inngest/route.ts's functions array (confirmed by reading the file), but Inngest's cloud platform never picked up this function from the deployment that shipped it (commit fb87015) — this project has no explicit Inngest-Vercel auto-sync integration confirmed configured, and no manual sync (PUT /api/inngest) was run after that deploy. This meant inngest.send({name: 'clio/template.fix_requested', ...}) succeeded from the app's side (no error anywhere in Vercel runtime logs, confirmed via get_runtime_errors — zero results) but nothing was subscribed to consume the event — a genuinely silent failure mode, not a crash, which is exactly why Arun saw zero progress with no error to point to. Diagnosed by querying template_library (Overlay: fix_state=generating, fix_attempt_count=0, fix_cycle_id set, fix_last_activity_at frozen since the initial feedback_received write) and template_fix_log (only feedback_received + Arun's own earlier nudge_status_check entry, never a single attempt_started row) directly against the live Supabase project, then confirming zero runtime errors in Vercel logs for the relevant window, then manually running `curl -X PUT https://distill-peach.vercel.app/api/inngest`, which returned {"message":"Successfully registered","modified":true} — the modified:true response proves the registry genuinely was out of date until this manual sync. Fix applied: the manual sync itself (no code change needed — the registration code was always correct). Follow-up for future deploys: any new Inngest function needs an explicit `curl -X PUT <prod-url>/api/inngest` (or a confirmed auto-sync integration) as a standard post-deploy step going forward, not just a code-level registration — this should be added to this project's own deploy checklist so it isn't silently missed again on a future job. Original stuck feedback text preserved for Arun's reference: 'Current template has the Header, subheader, canvas, within the canvas we have containers. increase the size of canvas as i see lot of white space below the canvas in the screen. the containers overlaps on the canvas. we need margins and spacing for all containers and it should not overlap on the canvas borders. font has to be minimum readable size for the user. increase font size and ensure font is taken care' — this feedback maps closely onto TMPL-02's already-shipped fixes (canvas margins/spacing, font floor) but was submitted specifically as Overlay-targeted feedback via TMPL-01's automated loop, so it will still run its own LLM-driven style-override fix cycle once retriggered, scoped to Overlay's own slot allowlist (colors, callout dimensions, panel border) rather than TMPL-02's renderer-level changes.

items.category: Template library review workflow (follow-on to RTV-04)
items.item: Reopen an approved template for additional feedback, re-review, and re-approval
items.description: Requested directly by Arun: an already-approved template had no way back into review — approval was a one-way door. He wanted to move it back to pending review, add more feedback, and approve again.
items.status: built, deployed 2026-07-11 — commit 5f23873, no new migration, no new route
items.comment: Feature Brief + Requirement Document: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/TMPL-03-reopen-approved-template.md, .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/TMPL-03-reopen-approved-template.md. This gap was explicitly flagged as out-of-scope in both RTV-04 and TMPL-02's specs — now closed. New PATCH action reopen_for_review, gated to rows currently status='approved' only (400 otherwise), moves status back to pending_review while clearing reviewed_by/reviewed_at/review_notes/fix_changes_summary/fix_failure_reason — but deliberately leaves style_overrides/sample_data/container_spec/fix_cycle_id/fix_attempt_count completely untouched, since style_overrides is the durable record of the template's approved visual state and TMPL-01's own fix cycles treat it as the starting point for incremental refinement, not something to discard. Single-click action, no confirmation dialog, no notes field, matching the existing 'Move back to Pending Review' precedent — reopening and leaving feedback are deliberately two separate steps, reusing the existing unmodified 'Request changes' flow (and TMPL-01's automated fix loop for Heatmap/Overlay) rather than duplicating that UI/API surface. The BA resolved the one real technical question — whether reopening an approved template could disrupt a live session already using it via RTV-05 — with direct code evidence: isTemplateApprovedForProduction() is an uncached, live read, but RTV-05's own session-level gate is computed once at first connect and persisted for the session's entire lifetime, never recomputed, so reopening has zero effect on any session already in progress and only affects new sessions connecting afterward. I independently verified this against RTV-05's actual code (having built and verified that gate myself earlier in this project) before approving. Independently verified before shipping: tsc clean, full test suite passing (269 tests, only the same pre-existing unrelated billing-test failure seen throughout this session), diff footprint matched exactly (2 application files + 1 test file), and read the actual PATCH route diff line-by-line to confirm the update payload genuinely excludes style_overrides/sample_data/container_spec/fix_cycle_id/fix_attempt_count.

items.category: Template library review workflow (follow-on to RTV-04)
items.item: Container overflow + minimum font size fix across the template library
items.description: Reported directly by Arun after reviewing templates: containers overflowing the screen (need margins/spacing so containers stay within canvas), and font too small on most templates (needs a minimum readable size on mobile and desktop). Routed through CEO/BA per his explicit instruction rather than patched ad hoc.
items.status: built, deployed 2026-07-11 — commit c3673b6, 4 root causes fixed across 25 files
items.comment: Feature Brief + Requirement Document: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/TMPL-02-container-overflow-and-font-size-fix.md, .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/TMPL-02-container-overflow-and-font-size-fix.md. Grounding: the BA actually rendered all 27 templates (a temporary, non-committed Vite harness importing the real renderer components + real mock data + real containerBudgets.ts maximums, since the admin page was blocked locally by a placeholder Clerk key) rather than guessing from source — every finding was empirically measured (getBoundingClientRect before/after, ReactFlow's live transform scale matrix, actual 375px mobile rendering) and I independently spot-checked the highest-impact claims myself against the real files before approving. Four distinct root causes, not one bug: (A) 15 templates' 'So what?' footer used absolute bottom-0 with no positioned ancestor anywhere in their own tree or the admin tool's preview card, so it escaped its card specifically in /dashboard/admin/templates (live sessions were already fine — SessionStack.tsx already wraps slides in relative) — fixed by adding relative to all 15 renderers plus the admin preview card as a defensive second layer, and giving the footer band a fixed h-[72px] with line-clamp-2 instead of unconstrained auto-grow. (B) 14 ReactFlow-based templates' fitView auto-zoom silently shrank text as content approached the existing character-budget maximums — measured directly on ComparisonTable: scale(1) at normal content down to scale(0.5) at true max-budget content, meaning already-small text-xs/text-sm rendered at an effective ~6-7px — fixed with a minZoom={0.85} floor plus a proportional node-height increase per template so the larger font has room without re-triggering the same shrink. (C) DefinitionTriptych/NarrativeCard used unconditional grid-cols-3 with no mobile breakpoint, confirmed overflowing both axes at a real 375px render — fixed with grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 (reusing ChevronProcess's existing breakpoint convention) plus overflow-y-auto for mobile scroll. (D) near-universal text-xs (12px) on substantive body copy across effectively all 27 templates, including RTV-04's own newest Heatmap/Overlay — raised to a text-sm (14px) floor, text-base (16px) preferred where layout has headroom, with short uppercase UI labels/badges (e.g. 'So what?', 'VERDICT', axis labels) explicitly and deliberately left at 12px per the spec's own named exception. containerBudgets.ts's character-budget numbers are explicitly unchanged — layout absorbs the font increase, not the other way around. A genuine pre-existing, unrelated bug was found and correctly left alone rather than silently fixed or silently ignored: ConceptMap's BranchNode reads a d.relationship field that doesn't exist on its data shape, so its 'description' text is currently invisible in the live UI regardless of font size — flagged in the spec for a separate ticket, not touched here (out of this CSS-only fix's scope). Also found and flagged, not deleted: components/templates/renderers/Hierarchy.tsx is imported but dead code — the 'Hierarchy' switch case actually renders HorizontalTree.tsx — left untouched per this project's no-delete-without-approval rule, flagged for a separate cleanup ticket. Independently verified before shipping: tsc clean, npm run build succeeded, full test suite passing (only the same pre-existing unrelated billing-test failure seen throughout this session), diff footprint matched exactly (25 files: 24 renderers + the admin preview client), and 3 of the highest-risk diffs (TwoByTwoMatrix's node-height/position recalculation, ConceptMap's confirmed-no-scope-creep change, and the position:relative fix pattern) read line-by-line myself. Confirmed zero touch to containerBudgets.ts, TMPL-01's fix-generator.ts/styleOverrideSlots.ts, Hierarchy.tsx, any API route, SessionStack.tsx, or WalkthroughClient.tsx.

items.category: Template library review workflow (follow-on to RTV-04)
items.item: Automated feedback-fix loop: Arun's template feedback triggers an LLM fix, mechanically validated, auto-resubmitted for re-review
items.description: Requested directly by Arun after reviewing RTV-04's workflow: 'Request changes' on Heatmap/Overlay previously only wrote a status+note with nothing acting on it. Now it fires an LLM-generated visual style fix, validated against a fixed slot allowlist before ever showing Arun a result, with a bounded 5-attempt auto-retry (Arun's own instruction: self-diagnose and retry on failure) and an uncapped manual 'force retrigger', plus a new per-template progress/console view with a 'nudge' action for when he's waited ~30 min and seen nothing.
items.status: built, deployed 2026-07-11 — commit fb87015, migration applied, scoped to Heatmap/Overlay only
items.comment: Feature Brief + Requirement Document: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/TMPL-01-automated-feedback-fix-loop.md, .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/TMPL-01-automated-feedback-fix-loop.md (CEO-approved after independent spot-checks against Heatmap.tsx/Overlay.tsx's real styling code, the RTV04_VALIDATED_TEMPLATES precedent, and the Inngest event-naming convention). Architecture: runtime style-override column (template_library.style_overrides), never an LLM-authored file edit — no deploy per fix, and structurally impossible for a fix cycle to write anywhere except one column of the one target template's row (no filesystem access, no git access anywhere in the mechanism). Scope enforcement (Layer 1/2, mirroring RTV-04's own two-layer pattern): a fixed per-template slot allowlist authored directly against the real renderer code (7 slots each for Heatmap/Overlay — intensity colors, cell size/gap for Heatmap; zone colors, callout dimensions, panel border for Overlay), closed-set color validation (only the existing 6 approved accent tokens, never an arbitrary hex), ranged-integer validation for dimensions, all-or-nothing (any unknown key or out-of-range value rejects the entire proposed fix, nothing partially applied). Bounded 5-attempt automatic retry per cycle, each attempt informed by the specific reason the prior one was rejected; an immediate terminal failure (not consuming remaining attempts) if the model itself reports the feedback implies a layout/content change rather than a styling change. A distinct fix_state='failed' terminal state — status never silently moves to pending_review on a failed cycle, verified by direct code trace: the failure-path DB update payloads structurally never contain a status key at all. Arun's manual 'Force retrigger' runs exactly one additional attempt (not a fresh 5-attempt loop) per his own spec's reasoning — safe because it requires his own deliberate action each time, unlike an unattended retry loop. A fix_cycle_id guard discards a stale/superseded invocation's result if a force-retrigger started a newer cycle while the old one was still running. New per-template Fix Progress view (/dashboard/admin/templates/[templateName]/progress) shows the full timestamped log, current attempt count, time since last update, and both nudge actions — gated by the same isConfiguredApprover() check as every other mutating action in this workflow. Two new accent color tokens (blue #3B82F6 for Pending Review, orange #F97316 for Fix Failed) wired in pending Arun's final confirmation — isolated to one file (app/dashboard/admin/templates/fixStatus.ts) for an easy swap if he wants different shades. Built across 3 agents (backend API/validation layer, scheduler Inngest job, frontend UI/renderer wiring) and independently verified as a combined whole after all three landed: tsc clean, npm run build succeeded, 733/735 tests passing (2 pre-existing unrelated billing-test failures, confirmed untouched), diff footprint matched all three reports, and the highest-risk diffs (the PATCH route's fix-trigger logic, the slot validator, the fix-cycle runner's status-never-touched-on-failure property, the nudge endpoint's auth gate, both renderer's override-application code) read line-by-line myself, not just trusted from the build reports. Confirmed the two real live-session TemplateRenderer call sites (SessionStack.tsx, KBSessionPreview.tsx) were untouched — this is purely an admin-review-tool feature. Scoped entirely to Heatmap/Overlay, matching the RTV04_VALIDATED_TEMPLATES precedent; the other 25 templates keep RTV-04's original request-changes behavior unchanged. Does not touch RTV-05's gate, isTemplateApprovedForProduction(), or any live-session wiring.

items.category: Live call behavior — RTV series (transcript-driven visualization)
items.item: Final phase: pre-fetch + dual-trigger display switch — makes the visualization actually follow Clio's live speech
items.description: Phase 5 (final) of the RTV series. Adds a pre-fetch trigger (generates the next topic's visualization content early, live, never cached) and a new title-detection-driven display switch alongside the existing show_visual-driven one, with a toggle deciding which is authoritative. Depends on RTV-02/03/04.
items.status: built, deployed 2026-07-10 — commit 3530afe, toggle OFF by default, migration applied. NOT enabled — gated on Arun's template approvals + RTV-03 accuracy evidence, neither satisfied yet
items.comment: Feature Brief + Requirement Document: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/RTV-05-prefetch-and-dual-trigger-display.md, .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/RTV-05-prefetch-and-dual-trigger-display.md (CEO-approved after independent spot-checks against the live codebase and a live Supabase query). Design: new env var NEXT_PUBLIC_RTV_DISPLAY_SWITCH_ENABLED, default OFF. Core deliverable is the anti-LIVE-06 dual-writer proof: a single boolean ref (rtv05DisplayActiveRef), assigned exactly once at connect from a session-level gate computed server-side (lib/content/rtv05-display-gate.ts) and never reassigned — the existing show_visual scroll_to write is gated on its negation, the new tracker-driven write on the flag itself, so exactly one writer is ever eligible per session. The session-level gate requires the toggle on, RTV-03's tracker active, AND every non-bookend template in the session individually approved via isTemplateApprovedForProduction() against the live template_library table — computed once at first connect and persisted (sessions.rtv05_display_active), never re-evaluated mid-session, specifically so a template's approval status changing mid-call (e.g. Arun editing it live in the admin UI) can never flip display authority mid-session. Pre-fetch reuses RTV-03's single tracker signal twice per hit (entering topic N kicks off pre-fetch for N+1; the next hit displays N+1 using whatever was staged) — a disclosed reinterpretation of the brainstorm's original framing, since RTV-03 only ever produces one signal type. New route POST /api/rtv05/prefetch-section (live generateTemplateData() + validateTemplateData() call, written only into that session's own walkthrough_state row, never cached elsewhere); new update_section_data command on the existing walkthrough-state route, following insert_section's established pattern. Migration 066 (sessions.rtv05_display_active, nullable boolean) applied to production. Independently verified before shipping (not just trusting the build report): tsc clean, 696/698 tests passing (2 pre-existing unrelated billing-test failures, confirmed untouched), diff footprint matched the report exactly, and the three highest-risk diffs (WalkthroughClient.tsx's toggle-guard wrapping, the display-gate module, the two API route changes) read line-by-line myself — all correct. Also spot-checked the build agent's factual claims directly: confirmed two distinct show_visual handlers exist (Hume-native vs ElevenLabs, only the former touched), confirmed insert_section has zero real call sites, confirmed APPROVAL_GATED_TEMPLATES is exactly {Heatmap, Overlay}. Verified live against Supabase (project nqxlpcshouboplhnuvrh) both before approving the spec and again after the build: all 27 template_library rows still pending_review, zero approved — the gate is confirmed structurally inert on today's data, not just asserted to be. Blocked: NEXT_PUBLIC_RTV_DISPLAY_SWITCH_ENABLED=false still needs manually adding to .env.local.example — same permission block as every prior RTV phase. Next, and only next step: Arun reviews/approves the 27 templates at /dashboard/admin/templates, and RTV-03's accuracy evaluator produces evidence meeting its bar — only then can this toggle be considered for enabling, and only for the specific templates approved.

items.category: Live call behavior — RTV series (transcript-driven visualization)
items.item: Template library + human-approval workflow: no visualization goes live without Arun's sign-off
items.description: Phase 4 of the RTV series. Adds 2 new template types (Heatmap, Overlay) and a template_library table with a pending_review/approved/changes_requested workflow plus an admin approval page. This is the hard, non-negotiable gate from the brainstorm (requirement #15) — no template design may ever render in a real live session without Arun personally approving it first.
items.status: fixed, deployed 2026-07-10 — commit e8fbccc, all 27 templates seeded at pending_review, migration applied
items.comment: Feature Brief + Requirement Document: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/RTV-04-template-library-and-approval.md, .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/RTV-04-template-library-and-approval.md. Design: new table template_library (migration 065, applied to production) seeded with all 27 template rows (25 pre-existing + Heatmap/Overlay) at status='pending_review' via a computed-rows script run against the real DB (local .env.local wasn't accessible in this environment, so the seed script's own pure helper logic was re-run standalone and inserted via the Supabase MCP directly — same 27 rows, same idempotent ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING). New admin page /dashboard/admin/templates, gated fail-closed by TEMPLATE_LIBRARY_APPROVER_EMAIL (unset = all approvals blocked, never fails open). Two real gaps caught during independent verification and fixed before shipping, not accepted as tradeoffs: (1) the build had wired Layer 1 structural validation (truncate-over-max, then retry-then-fall-back-to-mock-data if a field is under 40% of its max length) unconditionally into generateTemplateData() — the actual live content-generation path (session-content-pipeline.ts, session-plan.ts, KB regenerate routes) used for all 25 already-shipping templates today, not just the 2 new ones. A legitimate short value (a 'Yes'/'No' label, a one-word framework purpose) could have triggered real users' existing personalized content being silently replaced with generic mock data. Fixed by scoping validation to only Heatmap/Overlay via RTV04_VALIDATED_TEMPLATES in generator.ts — the 23 pre-existing templates keep byte-for-byte prior behavior. (2) isTemplateApprovedForProduction() existed and was unit-tested but had zero callers anywhere in the real pipeline — meaning Heatmap/Overlay were already selectable and renderable in a real live session (via selectTemplate()'s new keyword regexes, e.g. a topic literally about 'heat map' or 'where AI fits') with zero human sign-off, directly contradicting requirement #15. Fixed by adding selectApprovedTemplate() in selector.ts as the actual enforcement point, wired into both real call sites, falling back to DefinitionTriptych (the same default those topics would have hit before RTV-04 existed) until each template is individually approved. Still open, not yet resolved: containerBudgets.ts's 40%-minimum-length floor still applies uniformly to Heatmap/Overlay's own short label fields (e.g. a Heatmap row named 'Sales', legend labels) — this could cause unnecessary retry/mock-fallback churn on correct short content once these 2 templates go live; worth a spec refinement before RTV-05 wires actual display. Also blocked: TEMPLATE_LIBRARY_APPROVER_EMAIL=PLACEHOLDER_arun_email still needs manually adding to .env.local.example — that file is permission-blocked in this environment (same block the build agent hit). Next: Arun must review and approve/reject all 27 templates via the new admin page — RTV-05 (pre-fetch + dual-trigger display wiring) explicitly depends on this, not just the workflow being built.

items.category: Live call behavior — RTV series (transcript-driven visualization)
items.item: Observe-only live tracker: identify which topic Clio is teaching from her real-time speech
items.description: Phase 3 of the RTV series. Listens to Clio's live, improvised speech and concludes which of a session's known topics she's currently teaching, using RTV-02's golden-word markers — logs its conclusions only, never touches the display. Depends on RTV-02 (approved, merged, migration applied).
items.status: fixed, deployed 2026-07-10 — commit 328ad3b, toggle OFF by default, migration applied
items.comment: Feature Brief + Requirement Document: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/RTV-03-live-position-tracking.md, .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/RTV-03-live-position-tracking.md. Design: new env var NEXT_PUBLIC_RTV_TRACKING_ENABLED, default OFF, requires HUME_NATIVE_SUMMARY_MODE=true and the session's rtv_eligible=true to activate. Depth-2 lookahead state machine (checks current+1 and current+2 simultaneously) rather than strict depth-1 — a real correctness gap the BA caught: literally following 'a hit for the next topic's markers advances by one' would leave the tracker stuck forever if a single golden word is ever missed, violating the 'never more than one topic out of sync, self-corrects' requirement. A current+2 hit without a current+1 hit is logged as a gap_jump self-correction; depth is capped at exactly 2 so 'never jumps backward/speculatively skips ahead' holds by construction, not a runtime guard. Two new prompt rules (11, 12; PROMPT_TEMPLATE_VERSION bumped to v6) tell Clio to recap before each topic transition and explicitly say 'overview'/'summary' before those two bookend sections — the designed verbal cues the tracker listens for. Structurally observe-only: the tracker's refs (rtvStateRef, rtvTopicsRef) never touch the display-side refs (currentSectionIndexRef, sectionsRef) — the same isolation pattern already used for LIVE-01's liveConductorRef — and the tracker's only side effect is a fire-and-forget audit-log write (3 new event types), wrapped in its own try/catch so a tracker failure can never break the live call. A new sibling Inngest cron (same 15-min window as the existing FB-008 quality evaluator, reusing its transcript-fetch logic verbatim) replays each session's real Recall.ai transcript after the fact to compute an accuracy report (topics matched, max topics out of sync, timing deltas) against the tracker's own logged conclusions — reviewable via a new admin endpoint. This is the evidence a future phase needs before the tracker is ever allowed to control anything a user sees. One real issue caught and fixed during review (not accepted as a tradeoff): the tracker's shared tokenize() function was being imported from RTV-02's module, which has server-only imports (Anthropic SDK, Resend) at module scope — this would have transitively bundled both into the browser via WalkthroughClient.tsx. Fixed by extracting tokenize() into its own dependency-free module (lib/content/tokenize.ts). Built, independently verified (tsc clean, 31 new tests passing, full suite unaffected, grep-confirmed zero references to display-control APIs in the tracker module), committed and deployed 2026-07-10 (commit 328ad3b), migration 064 applied to production DB. Next phase (RTV-04, template library) requires Arun's personal sign-off on each template design before use — not yet started.

items.category: Live call behavior — RTV series (transcript-driven visualization)
items.item: Fail-closed pre-flight gate: block session start if Clio's transcript never reaches the app
items.description: Phase 1 of the RTV series (docs/brainstorm-realtime-transcript-driven-visualization.md). Arun's hard requirement: a live tracker (RTV-03) will depend on receiving Clio's transcript in real time — if that relay is dead, the session must not start at all, not run degraded. Fail closed, not fail open.
items.status: fixed, deployed 2026-07-10 — commit 09e5dd1, toggle OFF by default
items.comment: Feature Brief + Requirement Document: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/RTV-01-relay-preflight-gate.md, .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/RTV-01-relay-preflight-gate.md. Design: new env var NEXT_PUBLIC_RELAY_PREFLIGHT_GATE_ENABLED, default OFF. On a fresh Hume-native connect, billing (speak_verified) is deferred until the app confirms Clio's first non-empty transcribed utterance within 20s; if it never arrives, the session is torn down before any minutes bill, reverted to scheduled (relaunchable) via a new POST /api/sessions/relay-blocked endpoint, and the participant sees a calm 'Session Rescheduled' overlay (not the existing red 'Unable to Connect' modal — new overlay takes precedence). Also closes a latent hole where an empty assistant_message could fire billing today even with a dead relay. Built, independently verified (tsc clean, 12/12 new integration tests passing, diff scope matched exactly). One judgment call beyond the literal spec: added a `!sessionEndedRef.current` guard to the relay-confirmed handler to close a microscopic race the spec's timeout-side guard didn't symmetrically cover. Test-infra gap found and flagged separately (not fixed here, out of scope): this repo cannot currently mount a React component in vitest (missing @testing-library/dom peer + no @vitejs/plugin-react) — AC-1/AC-2's live happy-path/block-path behavior is verified by code trace + the endpoint test, not a mounted-component test; needs a real live Hume session to fully confirm.

items.category: Live call behavior — RTV series (transcript-driven visualization)
items.item: Content-authoring pipeline: generate unique 'golden word' markers per session topic
items.description: Phase 2 of the RTV series. Produces the marker data the future live tracker (RTV-03) will listen for — a specific named term per topic that appears nowhere else in that session, so a single hit is decisive. DATA ONLY in this phase: nothing runs live, nothing displays.
items.status: fixed, deployed 2026-07-10 — commit f00fa40, toggle OFF by default, migration applied
items.comment: Feature Brief + Requirement Document: .claude/agents/clio/feature-briefs/RTV-02-marker-generation-pipeline.md, .claude/agents/clio/requirement-docs/RTV-02-marker-generation-pipeline.md. Design: new env var RTV_MARKER_GENERATION_ENABLED, default OFF. A candidate word qualifies as a marker only if (1) it's a noun/specific named/technical term, not descriptive language, (2) it passes a 'cannot-miss' test — the topic can't be taught without saying it, (3) it appears in exactly ONE topic across the whole session, grouped by topic not raw count (a word repeated many times inside its own topic and never elsewhere is the strongest 'golden word', not disqualified — this was a real correction Arun made mid-brainstorm to the orchestrator's first draft of this rule). No fallback allowed: a topic that yields zero qualifying words after escalating through 3 source levels (the ~40-word summary line -> full TEACH text -> full 8-field article) flags the whole session rtv_eligible=false and alerts an admin, rather than ever shipping empty markers. Bookend sections (Overview/Summary) skip the algorithm and use their own section name as a literal marker. Storage: new additive columns sessions.session_markers (jsonb) + sessions.rtv_eligible (boolean), keyed by session UUID — CEO/BA correctly overrode the brief's suggested topic_content_cache location after finding it's empty for real Hume-native sessions. Migration 063_session_markers_rtv02.sql applied directly to production DB. Built, independently verified (tsc clean, 11/11 new unit tests passing, diff scope matched exactly, full existing suite unaffected). Marker source is deliberately Session Content only (the 'what to cover' text Clio actually teaches from), never the richer Topic Knowledge Base — a second explicit correction Arun made, since the KB contains reference material she may never say aloud.

items.item: Test toggle: real-time visualization vs pre-built KB content
items.description: Arun wants to A/B test the original real-time visualization generator (built before the KB/pre-built-content pipeline existed) against the current approach, during a real live Hume-native call. A simple on/off toggle for this.
items.status: deployed (off by default) — Arun hasn't chosen to flip it on yet
items.comment: Went through full CEO -> BA spec -> approval -> build process per Arun's explicit instruction (no shortcuts, even for small/test tooling). Off by default = zero behavior change anywhere, verified via direct code search (not assumption) — confirmed touches nothing outside this one toggle and its two call sites. On: the live call skips pre-built content and generates the visualization live instead. Explicitly disposable/test-only, not a permanent feature. Code is live in production but the env var is not set — needs NEXT_PUBLIC_REALTIME_VISUAL_TEST=true set in Vercel before Arun can actually test it.
